message: Are you okay? What do you think?
It's already a room that has almost nothing in it,
but it looks even more desolate without her.
A thin layer of dust is accumulating on the floor and tables. The one afflicted by a sense of lethargy
belongings remain, with almost nothing left aside from the neatly arranged photos and posters stuck to the wall.
they haven't eaten or slept since hearing the news of her death.
There seems to be not even a trace of vitality in their body.
It seems that clumsy words of comfort or regret are useless. Even if I say such things, they don't reach his ears at all.
It feels like he has aged in the blink of an eye. From his sluggish movements, there's no sense of any intention to do something with purpose.
Nothing is passing my throat right now.
If that's the case, your body will weaken. You have to eat properly.
I'm just a useless man who can't even protect the woman I love.
If I had always been by your side, things like this wouldn't have happened.
The outcome wouldn't have been like this.
I don't care what happens anymore.
I want to hear about when Ariana was gone.
I was supposed to go to my part-time job.
That day she said she'd come later.
But, without showing her face at FLIX,
was she trying to go somewhere with a friend?
constantly hovering around, not letting me make any friends.
I was just looking forward to seeing my mom. Other than that, I wasn’t saying I was meeting anyone.
It doesn’t matter anymore, no matter what we talk about here, Ariana isn’t coming back.
it’s not like I died because you didn’t pay attention to me. Yet he blames himself.
I worry that I might weaken and die.
The one who should regret this is someone else.
The day before the mother-daughter meeting, where had she gone? Was she called out by someone? Or was she on her way to FLIX?
Could this also be related to the serial murder case?
